children.name: Cnemophilus
children.description: A genus of stout, medium-sized passerines endemic to the montane forests of New Guinea, superficially resembling birds-of-paradise but now placed in their own family. Males are brilliantly coloured in iridescent blues, purples, and yellows, while females are dull olive-brown. Their diet consists primarily of fruit, and they build domed nests in tree forks.

children.name: Loboparadisea
children.description: A monotypic genus of satinbird endemic to the mountains of New Guinea, with the male possessing a bizarre fleshy yellow wattle around the bill. The genus name meaning 'lobe-paradise bird' reflects its historical misclassification as a bird-of-paradise. It is a frugivore of the mid-montane forests.
